## reference · gestalt similarity uniform treatment

* The principle of similarity states that items sharing a visual characteristic (color, shape, size) are perceived as related.
* Use a consistent visual style for each type of UI element to help users understand and use the design easily.
* **Color**:
	+ Use a shared color to signify related items and similar functionality.
	+ Reserve a single link color for interactive text and clickable elements.
	+ Use a separate color for primary calls-to-action to distinguish them from secondary buttons.
* **Shape**:
	+ Use a consistent shape to indicate grouping (e.g., rounded buttons, rectangular filters).
	+ Avoid using similar shapes for different categories or functions.
* **Size**:
	+ Use size to signal relationship and importance.
	+ Consistent size indicates same visual prominence and grouping.

Sources: https://www.nngroup.com/articles/gestalt-similarity/
